title: G3: Law of large numbers / centering constant with $o(n^{1/6})$ error

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
Need a rigorous LLN $L(\pi)/\sqrt n\to c(q_n)$ with the correct constant and quantitative error control, **uniform** over the relevant $q_n$ window.

- Fixed $q<1$: Bhatnagar--Peled give $c(q)$ explicitly, with $c(q)\to 2$ as $q\to1$.
- $q_n\to1$: to center at the fluctuation scale one needs $a_n=2\sqrt n(1+o(1))$ with error $o(n^{1/6})$ — far stronger than a bare LLN, since the fluctuations are of order $n^{1/6}$.

Gap: prove the LLN with explicit constant $c(q_n)$ and a quantitative correction term controlled to $o(n^{1/6})$ in the KPZ regime. Tools: Kingman subadditive ergodic theorem / superadditivity; Mallows limit-shape (Starr; Mueller--Starr) in the $q=1-\beta/n$ window. Beware: a mismatch between $a_n$ and the true mean of order $n^{1/6}$ destroys the TW limit.
